claims.safe_public_wording: The corrected one-shot consultation returned adapter_status=pond_backed.
claims.risk_if_overstated: Would imply broader runtime guarantees beyond this recorded consultation.

claims.safe_public_wording: The response included non-empty recalled motifs.
claims.risk_if_overstated: Would treat motif recall as independent validation of every proof claim.

claims.safe_public_wording: The response retrieved non-empty artifact and lineage refs.
claims.risk_if_overstated: Would treat retrieval as proof that all cited artifacts support stronger conclusions.

claims.safe_public_wording: The response preserved unresolved tensions and did not force them into a settled conclusion.
claims.risk_if_overstated: Would erase live uncertainty and overstate proof completeness.

claims.safe_public_wording: The response surfaced claim-pressure warnings that blocked unsupported and synthetic-inflation claims.
claims.risk_if_overstated: Would convert warning signals into validation evidence.

claims.safe_public_wording: Proof 010 validates only the synthetic fixture boundary.
claims.risk_if_overstated: Would imply real-trace or universal validation from a synthetic fixture.

claims.safe_public_wording: Proof 011 remains the unexecuted real-trace gate required before stronger public claims.
claims.risk_if_overstated: Would imply a real trace was selected or executed when it was not.

claims.claim: The one-shot run produced executable next validation.
claims.safe_public_wording: The run produced an executable real-trace replay gate plan with fields, pass/fail criteria, and disproof criteria.
claims.risk_if_overstated: Would imply the next validation has already passed.

claims.claim: The advantage claim is bounded to this run.
claims.safe_public_wording: A pond-backed advantage was observed for this single proof run compared with the prior placeholder baseline.
claims.risk_if_overstated: Would become an unsupported global superiority claim.
